rulings.Case Title: TALIA KENNEDY VS. META PLATFORMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay And For Protective Order To Preclude Deposition Of Non-Party Nancy Lowenkron
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2024, Line 1. DEFENDANT META PLATFORMS, INC. (F/K/A FACEBOOK, INC.)'s To Stay And For Protective Order To Preclude Deposition Of Non-Party Nancy Lowenkron. Defendant Meta Platforms, Inc.'s "motion to stay and for protective order to preclude deposition of non-party Nancy Lowenkron" is denied. Lowenkron is a Meta in-house lawyer; its motion makes three unavailing arguments for barring her deposition. First, Meta says Lowenkron's deposition would be "duplicative." (Memo. 2:8.) But virtually all depositions cover issues also covered by other deponents. Second, it is claimed "Lowenkron's involvement in this case was very limited." (Id.) But it is undisputed that Lowenkron was on Meta's conflicts committee and plaintiff was allegedly fired for conflicts. (Id. at 3:15-16, 4:25, 5:14-16.) Moreover, if Lowenkron's role was limited, her deposition should be short. Third, Meta says many of Lowenkron's communications were privileged (id. at 2:20-22); if so, appropriate instructions and objections may be made at her deposition. Finally, it is notable that Meta's legal authorities are largely out-of-jurisdiction and/or regard "opposing counsel," though Lowenkron is not included in the caption of defense counsel and is not claimed to be a trial team member. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JIA LIU VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2024, Line 2. PLAINTIFF JIA LIU's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents. Plaintiff's motion to compel is continued to January 30 to enable supplemental briefing. At the crux of this lemon law discovery dispute is a question neither side grapples with from a legal perspective: when a party is involved in many similar actions simultaneously, is it obliged to prepare a sufficient number of persons most knowledgeable to enable timely PMK depositions? Plaintiff served its first PMK notice in May 2024, but the earliest deposition date defendant has proffered was 11 months out, in April 2025. This defendant says - without legal citation - is because it does not have enough PMKs. (Opp. 6:10-18, 26; 9:15-18; 19:9-12.) But plaintiff says - also without legal citation - defendant "is acutely aware of the volume of cases filed each year yet has chosen not to hire/train/accommodate additional PMKs." (Rply. 3:11-13.) Each side shall file and serve a legal brief (maximum five pages) on this issue by January 15. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: OMID KORDESTANI VS. X CORP. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2024, Line 4. DEFENDANT X CORP. Motion For A Protective Order. X Corp.'s motion for protective order is denied. California law requires litigants to meet and confer regarding "each issue presented by" a discovery motion before bringing it to court. (CCP 2016.040.) Here, the meet-and-confer was limited to the number of requests for admission while X's motion is broader. (Rply. 3:23-26; Opp. 6:10-16.) For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH MATUSEK-BLEECKER VS. SURGICAL CARE AFFILIATES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2024, Line 5. 1 - DEFENDANTS SURGICAL CARE AFFILIATES, LLC, AND MARY FLYNN's Amended DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to two counts of the complaint - (7) Labor Code 1102.5 and (9) intentional infliction of emotional distress (IIED) - is sustained with leave to amend. As to Labor Code 1102.5, "facts in support of each of the requirements of a statute upon which a cause of action is based must be specifically pled." (Fisher v. San Pedro Peninsula Hospital (1989) 214 Cal.App.3d 590, 604.) Here, the "protected complaint" specifically pled was to two "fellow registered nurses" (Cmplt. 6:3-11), but section 1102.5 requires disclosure "to a person with authority over the employee or another employee who has the authority to investigate" (Id.) As to the IIED claim, the pled conduct must be "extreme and outrageous." (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1050.) Plaintiff cites no authority holding that conduct of the sort pled here is extreme and outrageous. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
